summary: Adding validate_nesting param to Context for nested context validation to prevent resource leaks

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/shell_completion.py,src/click/core.py
summary: Fixing resource leak in shell completion by wrapping make_context calls in with-statements in _resolve_context

type: STATUS
files: src/click/shell_completion.py:_resolve_context,src/click/core.py:Context.exit docstring
summary: Done: wrapped make_context calls in with-statements inside _resolve_context to close contexts/file handles during shell completion; only touched Context.exit()'s docstring in core.py (versionchanged note), no other core.py changes -- should not conflict with your validate_nesting work unless you also edit _resolve_context or Context.exit

type: STATUS
files: src/click/core.py:Context.__init__/close/_make_sub_context,src/click/shell_completion.py:_resolve_context
summary: Added validate_nesting param+tracking to Context; in _resolve_context I added validate_nesting=False to the 3 make_context/cli.make_context calls (ctx_args and two inline calls) - please merge my validate_nesting=False kwarg into your with-statement wrapping of those same calls so both features coexist
